_comment: Bridge file that solves the vocabulary mismatch between indianDistrictData.json soil types and cropDetails.json soil types. Three-layer mapping: District raw types → User-facing standardized options → Crop-compatible soil type keywords.

userFacingOptions.label: Black Cotton
userFacingOptions.description: Dark, clayey, cracks in dry season — common in Maharashtra, MP, AP

userFacingOptions.label: Red Soil
userFacingOptions.description: Reddish due to iron oxide, moderately fertile

userFacingOptions.label: Laterite
userFacingOptions.description: Acidic, iron-rich, found in Western Ghats, NE India

userFacingOptions.label: Sandy
userFacingOptions.description: Coarse, low water retention, found in arid zones

userFacingOptions.label: Sandy Loam
userFacingOptions.description: Mix of sand and loam — good drainage, moderate fertility

userFacingOptions.label: Loam
userFacingOptions.description: Balanced sand/silt/clay — ideal for most crops

userFacingOptions.label: Clay
userFacingOptions.description: Very fine texture, high water retention, slow drainage

districtToCropSoilMap._comment: Maps district JSON soil_types[] values → user-facing option IDs. When a district is selected, use this to pre-suggest the soil type in the UI form.

userFacingToCropSoils._comment: Maps user-facing soil option IDs → list of soil type strings as they appear in cropDetails.json suitable_soil_types. Matching is done by checking if any crop soil type CONTAINS or IS CONTAINED BY any string in this list (case-insensitive).

broadCompatibilitySoils._comment: These soil type keywords in cropDetails indicate the crop tolerates a wide variety of soils. Give a partial match score (0.6) even if primary mapping doesn't match.
